name: Diabetes mellitus
description: Diabetes mellitus — chronic disorder of high blood glucose; all types.

en.name: Diabetes mellitus
en.shortDescription: Diabetes mellitus — chronic disorder of high blood glucose; all types.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Diabetes mellitus is a chronic metabolic disorder of persistently high blood glucose, from absolute insulin deficiency (type 1) or insulin resistance with relative deficiency (type 2), among other forms. In intensive care it is a common comorbidity that affects glucose control, infection risk, and the management of many acute illnesses.

## Scope

The set is anchored on the SNOMED concept *201820 / 73211009 — Diabetes mellitus* with all descendants included, so it captures **all types** (type 1, type 2, secondary, gestational, and others) and diabetes with complications.

## Mapping Notes

- All documented types of diabetes map here; the set records the presence of diabetes, not its type or control.
- Diabetes that has caused chronic kidney disease maps here **and** to the Chronic kidney disease set (the two variables are recorded independently).
- Transient stress hyperglycaemia without a diabetes diagnosis is not part of this set.
